title: phaca beckwithii
side_effects: Phaca beckwithii can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues. Ingesting the plant's leaves or flowers can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure to its toxic compounds may lead to kidney damage or failure. Skin contact with the plant's sap can cause redness, itching, and blistering, while inhaling its volatile oils can trigger respiratory problems, including asthma attacks. If not handled carefully, Phaca beckwithii can also lead to headaches, dizziness, and fatigue due to its psychoactive properties.
